articles.title: Weather Today [10 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Snow & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Jammu and Kashmir
articles.full_text:
Weather Today, 10 May 2026: India is witnessing a dramatic weather split this May as northern and western states battle extreme heat while southern and eastern regions prepare for thunderstorms, lightning and heavy rain. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), temperatures in parts of Rajasthan and Gujarat have already crossed 44°C, while hill states continue to experience cold winds and isolated snowfall while this unusual contrast highlights how rapidly India’s climate patterns are shifting during the pre-monsoon season.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
The Himalayan belt is expected to remain cooler than the plains over the next few days as Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand may witness light rainfall and isolated snowfall in higher reaches because of an incoming Western Disturbance.
In Srinagar, temperatures are expected between Minimum at 9°C & Maximum at 19°C, while cloudy skies and cool winds may dominate the evening hours. Tourist areas in Gulmarg and Sonamarg may receive light snow showers at higher altitudes.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
Meteorologists say several atmospheric systems are driving the sharp rise in temperatures. Dry northwesterly winds, reduced cloud cover and delayed moisture penetration are intensifying heat accumulation over northwestern India. The IMD has warned that temperatures across Delhi, Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh may rise by 3°C to 5°C over the next seven days. Rajasthan’s Phalodi recently recorded 44.8°C, among the highest temperatures in the country this season while urban heat, concrete expansion and rising humidity are also worsening discomfort levels in major cities.
Where are the Heat Wave Alerts?
- West Rajasthan: Severe heat wave warning for 7 days
- Punjab and Haryana: Hot and dry conditions intensifying
- Vidarbha Region: Dry heat and strong sunlight expected
Delhi-NCR Weather Today
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 39°C
Delhi-NCR is expected to experience mainly clear skies with intense afternoon heat and wind speeds may remain around 3.5 km/h, while temperatures could climb to 45°C later this week. Pollution and dry winds are expected to worsen heat discomfort.
Chennai Weather Today
Minimum: 28°C | Maximum: 36°C
Chennai may witness partly cloudy skies with chances of evening thunderstorms and humidity levels will remain high, increasing discomfort despite moderate coastal winds.
Mumbai Weather Today
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 34°C
Mumbai will continue experiencing humid weather conditions with sea breeze influence and cloud cover may increase slightly, though heavy rainfall is unlikely today.
Hyderabad Weather Today
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 36°C
Hyderabad is likely to witness gusty winds and isolated thunderstorms later in the evening due to developing pre-monsoon activity.
Karnataka Weather Today
Minimum: 24°C | Maximum: 35°C
Coastal and interior Karnataka may receive scattered rain accompanied by lightning and wind speeds between 30 and 50 km/h.
Uttar Pradesh Weather Today
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 37°C
Lucknow and nearby regions are expected to remain hot and dry while temperatures may cross 43°C by midweek under strong sunlight.
Rajasthan Weather Today
Minimum: 22°C | Maximum: 44°C
Rajasthan remains the hottest region in India as Jaipur, Jodhpur and Phalodi continue to face dangerous heat wave conditions with dry desert winds intensifying daytime exposure.
Jammu and Kashmir Weather Today
Minimum: 9°C | Maximum: 19°C
Pleasant daytime conditions are expected across Srinagar Valley, though higher reaches may experience snowfall and light rain.
Punjab Weather Today
Minimum: 24°C | Maximum: 37°C
Punjab is expected to remain extremely warm, with Chandigarh temperatures likely to rise toward 44°C later this week.
Ahmedabad Weather Today
Minimum: 29°C | Maximum: 44°C
Ahmedabad continues to remain under severe heat stress with extremely hot afternoons and warm nights reducing nighttime cooling.
City-Wise Sunset & Sunrise Timing
Full City-Wise Rain Forecast Across Major Cities
How Will Rising Temperatures Impact?
Rising temperatures may trigger dehydration, heatstroke, electricity demand spikes and water shortages across urban regions. Health experts warn that children, senior citizens and outdoor workers remain most vulnerable.
Agriculture may also suffer as soil moisture declines rapidly in heat wave zones and electricity consumption is already rising sharply in several states because of increased cooling demand.
Delhi NCR Weekly Weather Forecast
Delhi’s Air Quality Today – May 10, 2026
Delhi’s air quality remains in the “Poor” category due to rising dust particles, dry winds and vehicle emissions where as heat accumulation is preventing pollutants from dispersing efficiently.
Major Cities Air Quality Across India
FAQ’s: All About Today’s Weather
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
Yes. IMD has advised avoiding outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours in heat wave regions and staying indoors during thunderstorms.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
Yes. Northwest India may witness a 3°C to 5°C rise in temperatures this week.
3. Will there be any rain today?
Rain and thunderstorms are expected across parts of South India, eastern India and Himalayan regions.
4. Where will it snow today?
Higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh may witness isolated snowfall.
5. Which city is expected to remain hottest?
Rajasthan’s Phalodi and Ahmedabad are among the hottest regions, with temperatures touching nearly 45°C.
